arguments.query: HubSpot API documentation authentication OAuth2 API key developer portal
HubSpot developer API pricing gated self-serve free tier
HubSpot MCP server model context protocol
HubSpot developer account signup free API credentials self-serve
HubSpot API reference REST endpoints CRM marketing sales

researched_category.value: CRM / Marketing, Sales, and Customer Service Platform
researched_category.notes: HubSpot is a Smart CRM platform with Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, Content Hub, and Operations Hub.

one_liner.value: HubSpot is an all-in-one CRM platform that unifies marketing, sales, and customer service with integrated customer data, automation, analytics, and AI capabilities.
one_liner.notes: The developer platform description calls it a 'Smart CRM' with hubs for marketing, sales, service, content, and operations.

methods.notes: HubSpot supports OAuth 2.0 (primary for multi-account apps), private app access tokens (bearer tokens for single-account use), developer API keys (hapikey query param), and client credentials (OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant for webhooks journal). Static auth tokens are also available.

access.notes: Anyone can create a free HubSpot account and get API access. The developer platform explicitly states: 'No. You can create a free HubSpot account and build apps without a paid subscription.' Free tier includes API access with rate limits (100 req/10s, up to 650K daily). Developer test accounts are provided.

summary.value: HubSpot exposes a comprehensive REST API covering CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals, tickets, etc.), engagements (calls, emails, meetings, notes, tasks), marketing events, content (pages, blogs), e-commerce (carts, orders, products), properties, search, pipelines, associations, and more. APIs use date-based versioning (2026-03) alongside legacy v3/v4 paths. Webhooks are supported for event-driven integrations. The API surface is extremely broad, spanning all HubSpot hubs.
summary.notes: The CRM APIs reference lists 30+ object types with full CRUD operations. The HubSpot MCP server documentation confirms read/write access to all major CRM objects and engagements.

agent_interface.notes: HubSpot offers an official remote MCP server (available in public beta since May 2025) at mcp.hubspot.com, plus a Developer MCP server (local/CLI-based). Multiple community MCP servers exist on GitHub (shinzo-labs/hubspot-mcp, baryhuang/mcp-hubspot, etc.). Composio has a HubSpot toolkit. The official MCP server supports CRM objects read/write and read-only access to organizational context, marketing, and content.

rationale.value: HubSpot is fully buildable into an agent toolkit today. It has a comprehensive public REST API with excellent documentation, multiple authentication options (OAuth 2.0 being the recommended primary), self-serve free developer accounts with no gating or paid plan requirement, and an official MCP server already in public beta. Community MCP servers and Composio integration also exist. There are no material blockers to building an agent toolkit.
rationale.notes: All prerequisites for an agent toolkit are met: public API, free self-serve credentials, OAuth 2.0, official MCP server, and broad API surface.

s1.quote: Craft seamless experiences that help businesses grow better powered by HubSpot's Smart CRM
s1.title: HubSpot Developers

s2.quote: There are two authentication types available based on how you plan to distribute your app: OAuth is required for multiple accounts, while static auth access tokens are used for installing in a single account at a time.
s2.title: Authentication overview - HubSpot docs

s3.quote: Learn more about using OAuth or private access tokens to make authenticated API requests to HubSpot.
s3.title: HubSpot APIs | Authentication methods on HubSpot

s4.quote: HubSpot offers two distinct MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers — one for connecting any MCP-compatible AI tool or agent to your CRM, and one for building on the HubSpot Developer Platform.
s4.title: HubSpot MCP Server

s5.quote: No. You can create a free HubSpot account and build apps without a paid subscription. You'll get access to a developer test account with sample data for testing.
s5.title: Developer Platform Basics & Onboarding

s6.quote: Free and Starter plans: 100 requests per 10 seconds with a 650,000 daily cap. Professional and Enterprise: 190 requests per 10 seconds with 1,000,000 daily calls.
s6.title: HubSpot API Explained: Automate CRM Tasks Like a Pro

s7.quote: HubSpot’s CRM is a system for managing customer relationships and data. Learn about objects and the related CRM APIs.
s7.title: Understanding the CRM APIs - HubSpot docs

s8.quote: 2026-03 API reference. Reference documentation for HubSpot's date-versioned APIs, introduced with the 2026-03 release.
s8.title: 2026-03 API reference - HubSpot docs

s9.quote: HubSpot offers Marketing, Sales, Service, and CMS APIs alongside the CRM endpoints.
s9.title: HubSpot API Explained: Automate CRM Tasks Like a Pro

s10.quote: HubSpot's comprehensive offerings include the Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, and CMS Hub.
s10.title: HubSpot API Integration Guide: CRM, Contacts, Deals & ...

s11.quote: The HubSpot MCP server is now available in public beta. It allows AI clients such as Cursor and Claude to interact with HubSpot data.
s11.title: The HubSpot MCP Server - available in Public Beta

s12.quote: HubSpot. HubSpot is an inbound marketing, sales, and customer service platform integrating CRM, email automation, and analytics.
s12.title: HubSpot - Composio Toolkit | Composio

limitations: Pricing page (hubspot.com/pricing) failed to fetch - content not accessible via URL fetch tool. However, secondary sources confirmed free tier availability.
The official HubSpot MCP server is in public beta (announced May 2025), not yet GA, but it is functional and documented.
Some API endpoints may require specific plan tiers (e.g., certain marketing or sales APIs may need paid plans), but CRM API is available on all plans including free.
